The objective of this study was to formulate a biostable methacrylate-free 3-step total-etch adhesive (EA) with the goal of exceeding or achieving the as-prepared values of both an in-house formulated (IH-MA) and commercial (C-MA, Adper Sc", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Following screening, the candidate EA and C-MA were subjected to either thermocycling (10,000 cycles, 5-50°C) or 1 year of biochemical aging...", "direct_quote": "Following screening, the candidate EA and C-MA were subjected to either thermocycling (10,000 cycles, 5-50°C) or 1 year of biochemical aging in simulated human salivary esterase (SHSE) media using miniature short-rod specimens and evaluated for chang", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "RESULTS
A candidate EA (EA 5) was identified through formulation screening that achieved comparable (p > 0.05) physical and adhesive (µTBS a...", "direct_quote": "RESULTS
A candidate EA (EA 5) was identified through formulation screening that achieved comparable (p > 0.05) physical and adhesive (µTBS and KIC) properties to that of the C-MA immediately post-fabrication.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
